title: Atomic Habits
subtitle: An Easy & Proven Way to Build Good Habits & Break Bad Ones
author: James Clear
description: A revolutionary system for getting 1% better every day. James Clear reveals practical strategies for forming good habits, breaking bad ones, and mastering the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: Nobel laureate Daniel Kahneman takes us on a groundbreaking tour of the mind and explains the two systems that drive the way we think — fast intuitive thinking and slow deliberate thinking.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A magical story about Santiago, an Andalusian shepherd boy who dreams of traveling the world in search of treasure. His journey teaches him to listen to his heart and follow his dreams.

title: Sapiens
subtitle: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: Yuval Noah Harari boldly traces the entire history of humankind, from the Stone Age to the Silicon Age, exploring how biology and history have defined us and enhanced our understanding of what it means to be human.

title: Zero to One
subtitle: Notes on Startups, or How to Build the Future
author: Peter Thiel
description: Peter Thiel presents a radically new way to think about innovation and startups. Instead of copying what works, create something entirely new — go from zero to one.

title: Deep Work
subtitle: Rules for Focused Success in a Distracted World
author: Cal Newport
description: Cal Newport argues that the ability to concentrate without distraction on a cognitively demanding task is becoming both increasingly rare and increasingly valuable.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor E. Frankl
description: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl's memoir of life in Nazi death camps and its lessons for spiritual survival. He argues that we cannot avoid suffering but we can choose how to cope with it and find meaning in it.

title: The Lean Startup
subtitle: How Today's Entrepreneurs Use Continuous Innovation
author: Eric Ries
description: Eric Ries defines a scientific approach to creating and managing startups. Learn how to drive a startup by steering, adapting, and adjusting before it's too late.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: The private thoughts of the world's most powerful man, Roman Emperor Marcus Aurelius. His meditations on Stoic philosophy offer timeless wisdom on virtue, duty, and living a good life.

title: The Psychology of Money
subtitle: Timeless Lessons on Wealth, Greed, and Happiness
author: Morgan Housel
description: Morgan Housel shares 19 short stories exploring the strange ways people think about money and teaches you how to make better sense of one of life's most important topics.

title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
author: Dale Carnegie
description: The classic guide that has helped millions of people build better relationships, communicate effectively, and influence others positively.

title: Mindset
subtitle: The New Psychology of Success
author: Carol S. Dweck
description: Stanford psychologist Carol Dweck reveals how a simple idea about the brain — whether you believe intelligence is fixed or can grow — can create a love of learning and resilience.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
subtitle: A Counterintuitive Approach to Living a Good Life
author: Mark Manson
description: Mark Manson argues that improving our lives hinges not on our ability to turn lemons into lemonade, but on learning to stomach lemons better.

title: Start with Why
subtitle: How Great Leaders Inspire Everyone to Take Action
author: Simon Sinek
description: Simon Sinek explores how leaders can inspire cooperation, trust, and change. It all starts with WHY — understanding the purpose behind what you do.

title: Emotional Intelligence
subtitle: Why It Can Matter More Than IQ
author: Daniel Goleman
description: Daniel Goleman's groundbreaking work on the importance of emotional intelligence — self-awareness, self-regulation, motivation, empathy, and social skills — for success in life.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
subtitle: What the Rich Teach Their Kids About Money
author: Robert T. Kiyosaki
description: Robert Kiyosaki shares the story of his two dads — his real father and the father of his best friend — and the ways in which both men shaped his thoughts about money and investing.

title: Daring Greatly
subtitle: How the Courage to Be Vulnerable Transforms the Way We Live
author: Brené Brown
description: Brené Brown challenges everything we think we know about vulnerability, arguing that it is not weakness but our greatest measure of courage.

title: The Power of Now
subtitle: A Guide to Spiritual Enlightenment
author: Eckhart Tolle
description: Eckhart Tolle's message is simple: living in the now is the truest path to happiness and enlightenment. He shows how to quiet the mind and live in the present moment.

title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
author: Stephen R. Covey
description: Stephen Covey presents a principle-centered approach for solving personal and professional problems. His holistic, integrated approach gives you the security to adapt to change.

title: Creative Confidence
subtitle: Unleashing the Creative Potential Within Us All
author: Tom Kelley & David Kelley
description: IDEO founders Tom and David Kelley reveal how each of us can be more creative — in our work and personal lives — through building creative confidence.

title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
subtitle: Building a Business When There Are No Easy Answers
author: Ben Horowitz
description: Ben Horowitz offers essential advice on building and running a startup — practical wisdom for managing the toughest problems business school doesn't cover.

title: Shoe Dog
subtitle: A Memoir by the Creator of Nike
author: Phil Knight
description: Phil Knight shares the inside story of building Nike from a $50 loan to a $30 billion company — the risks, setbacks, and triumphs that made it possible.

title: The Power of Habit
subtitle: Why We Do What We Do in Life and Business
author: Charles Duhigg
description: Charles Duhigg explores the science behind habit creation and reformation. He reveals why habits exist, how they can be changed, and the impact they have on our lives.

title: Never Split the Difference
subtitle: Negotiating As If Your Life Depended On It
author: Chris Voss
description: Former FBI hostage negotiator Chris Voss shares field-tested tools for high-stakes negotiations. His techniques work in any situation — from business deals to personal relationships.

title: The Four Agreements
subtitle: A Practical Guide to Personal Freedom
author: Don Miguel Ruiz
description: Based on ancient Toltec wisdom, Don Miguel Ruiz reveals four simple agreements that can rapidly transform our lives: be impeccable with your word, don't take anything personally, don't make assumptions, always do your best.

title: Can't Hurt Me
subtitle: Master Your Mind and Defy the Odds
author: David Goggins
description: David Goggins shares his astonishing life story and reveals how mastering your mind can help you push past limits you never thought possible.

title: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
description: Sun Tzu's ancient masterpiece on military strategy has been applied to business, politics, and everyday life for centuries. Its wisdom on competition, leadership, and strategy remains timeless.

title: The 48 Laws of Power
author: Robert Greene
description: Robert Greene distills 3,000 years of the history of power into 48 essential laws. Each law is illustrated through historical examples from the lives of strategists, seducers, and con artists.

title: Ikigai
subtitle: The Japanese Secret to a Long and Happy Life
author: Héctor García & Francesc Miralles
description: Discover the Japanese concept of ikigai — your reason for being. This book reveals the secrets of the world's longest-living people and their approach to finding purpose and happiness.

title: The Obstacle Is the Way
subtitle: The Timeless Art of Turning Trials into Triumph
author: Ryan Holiday
description: Ryan Holiday shows how obstacles and adversity are actually the path to success, drawing on Stoic philosophy and historical examples to demonstrate how challenges make us stronger.

title: Steal Like an Artist
subtitle: 10 Things Nobody Told You About Being Creative
author: Austin Kleon
description: Austin Kleon shares transformative principles for creative work and life, showing that all creative work builds on what came before and that embracing influence is key to originality.

title: Big Magic
subtitle: Creative Living Beyond Fear
author: Elizabeth Gilbert
description: Elizabeth Gilbert shows us how to uncover the strange jewels that are hidden within each of us, embracing a life driven more by curiosity than fear.

title: The War of Art
subtitle: Break Through the Blocks and Win Your Inner Creative Battles
author: Steven Pressfield
description: Steven Pressfield identifies the enemy that every creator faces — Resistance — and shows how to overcome it to do your best work.

title: Outliers
subtitle: The Story of Success
author: Malcolm Gladwell
description: Malcolm Gladwell examines what makes high-achievers different, revealing how success is shaped by culture, timing, and opportunity more than we realize.

title: Grit
subtitle: The Power of Passion and Perseverance
author: Angela Duckworth
description: Angela Duckworth shows that the secret to outstanding achievement is not talent but a special blend of passion and persistence she calls grit.

title: Influence
subtitle: The Psychology of Persuasion
author: Robert B. Cialdini
description: Dr. Robert Cialdini reveals the six universal principles of influence and how to use them ethically in business and everyday life.

title: 12 Rules for Life
subtitle: An Antidote to Chaos
author: Jordan B. Peterson
description: Jordan Peterson provides twelve profound and practical principles for how to live a meaningful life, drawing on philosophy, psychology, history, and personal experience.

title: The Intelligent Investor
subtitle: The Definitive Book on Value Investing
author: Benjamin Graham
description: Benjamin Graham's timeless book on value investing has been the stock market bible since its publication. It teaches the fundamentals of sound investment strategy.

title: Think and Grow Rich
author: Napoleon Hill
description: Napoleon Hill's classic reveals the money-making secrets of Andrew Carnegie and other millionaires of his era. It teaches the philosophy of personal achievement.

title: Quiet
subtitle: The Power of Introverts in a World That Can't Stop Talking
author: Susan Cain
description: Susan Cain argues that we dramatically undervalue introverts and shows how much we lose in doing so. She charts the rise of the Extrovert Ideal throughout the twentieth century.

title: The ONE Thing
subtitle: The Surprisingly Simple Truth Behind Extraordinary Results
author: Gary Keller
description: Gary Keller teaches you how to cut through clutter and focus on what matters most. By narrowing your focus to one thing, you can achieve extraordinary results.

title: The Compound Effect
subtitle: Jumpstart Your Income, Your Life, Your Success
author: Darren Hardy
description: Darren Hardy shows how small, everyday decisions will lead you to the life you've always desired. Success is built on small consistent actions compounded over time.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: Stephen Hawking's landmark work explores the big bang, black holes, and the nature of time itself — making complex cosmology accessible to anyone curious about the universe.

title: Ego Is the Enemy
author: Ryan Holiday
description: Ryan Holiday shows how ego is the invisible enemy at every stage of success — from aspiring to succeeding to failing — and how to master it.

title: Drive
subtitle: The Surprising Truth About What Motivates Us
author: Daniel H. Pink
description: Daniel Pink reveals the surprising science of motivation, arguing that the secret to high performance is our deeply human need for autonomy, mastery, and purpose.

title: Essentialism
subtitle: The Disciplined Pursuit of Less
author: Greg McKeown
description: Greg McKeown introduces a systematic discipline for discerning what is absolutely essential, then eliminating everything that is not, so we can make the highest possible contribution.

title: The Body Keeps the Score
subtitle: Brain, Mind, and Body in the Healing of Trauma
author: Bessel van der Kolk
description: Dr. Bessel van der Kolk uses recent scientific advances to show how trauma reshapes both body and brain, compromising sufferers' capacities for pleasure, engagement, and trust.

title: Good to Great
subtitle: Why Some Companies Make the Leap and Others Don't
author: Jim Collins
description: Jim Collins and his research team identify what makes good companies become great ones — revealing the factors that separate the best from the merely good.

title: Into the Wild
author: Jon Krakauer
description: The true story of Christopher McCandless, who gave up everything to live in the Alaskan wilderness. A compelling meditation on the lure of adventure and the cost of idealism.

title: Flow
subtitle: The Psychology of Optimal Experience
author: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi
description: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi reveals how the state of flow — being completely absorbed in an activity — can lead to optimal experience and happiness.

title: Educated
subtitle: A Memoir
author: Tara Westover
description: Tara Westover's remarkable memoir of growing up in a survivalist family in Idaho and her journey to education. A testament to the transformative power of learning.

title: Becoming
author: Michelle Obama
description: Michelle Obama's intimate memoir chronicles her journey from the South Side of Chicago to the White House, exploring her triumphs and disappointments, both public and private.

title: Range
subtitle: Why Generalists Triumph in a Specialized World
author: David Epstein
description: David Epstein examines the world's most successful people and reveals that generalists — not specialists — are primed to excel in our complex world.

title: Think Again
subtitle: The Power of Knowing What You Don't Know
author: Adam Grant
description: Adam Grant explores the critical art of rethinking: learning to question your opinions and open other people's minds. It can be the key to professional and personal success.

author: George Orwell
description: George Orwell's dystopian masterpiece about a totalitarian society where Big Brother is always watching. A chilling warning about the dangers of totalitarianism and the erosion of truth.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: Harper Lee's Pulitzer Prize-winning novel about innocence, justice, and moral growth in the American South. Told through the eyes of young Scout Finch, it remains one of the most beloved novels ever written.

title: The 5 AM Club
subtitle: Own Your Morning, Elevate Your Life
author: Robin Sharma
description: Robin Sharma reveals a morning routine that has helped his clients maximize their productivity, activate their best health, and bulletproof their serenity in this age of complexity.

title: Attached
subtitle: The New Science of Adult Attachment
author: Amir Levine & Rachel Heller
description: Using attachment theory, this book helps you identify your attachment style and understand how it shapes your relationships, providing a road map for building lasting love.

title: The 5 Love Languages
subtitle: The Secret to Love That Lasts
author: Gary Chapman
description: Dr. Gary Chapman reveals five distinct ways people express and experience love. Understanding your love language — and your partner's — can transform your relationship.

title: The Courage to Be Disliked
subtitle: How to Free Yourself, Change Your Life, and Achieve Real Happiness
author: Ichiro Kishimi & Fumitake Koga
description: Using the theories of Alfred Adler, this book presents a dialogue between a philosopher and a young man that challenges fundamental assumptions about happiness and freedom.

title: 21 Lessons for the 21st Century
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: Yuval Noah Harari addresses the most urgent issues of our time — from AI and fake news to immigration and terrorism — offering a framework for understanding the present.

title: The Daily Stoic
subtitle: 366 Meditations on Wisdom, Perseverance, and the Art of Living
author: Ryan Holiday
description: Ryan Holiday offers 366 days of Stoic insights and exercises, drawing on the wisdom of Marcus Aurelius, Seneca, and Epictetus for a more resilient life.

title: Rework
author: Jason Fried & David Heinemeier Hansson
description: The founders of Basecamp share their unconventional approach to running a business: ignore the conventional wisdom, skip the business plan, and start making something.

title: Extreme Ownership
subtitle: How U.S. Navy SEALs Lead and Win
author: Jocko Willink & Leif Babin
description: Two Navy SEAL officers who led the most decorated special operations unit of the Iraq War demonstrate how to apply Seal leadership lessons to business and life.

title: The Design of Everyday Things
author: Don Norman
description: Don Norman explores the principles of good design and how the design of everyday objects affects our lives. A classic that changed how designers think about user experience.

title: Born a Crime
subtitle: Stories from a South African Childhood
author: Trevor Noah
description: Trevor Noah tells the compelling, sometimes hilarious story of growing up in South Africa during and after apartheid. His existence as a mixed-race child was literally a crime.

title: Make Your Bed
subtitle: Little Things That Can Change Your Life...And Maybe the World
author: Admiral William H. McRaven
description: Based on a viral commencement speech, Admiral McRaven shares ten principles he learned during Navy SEAL training that helped him overcome challenges and achieve success.

title: So Good They Can't Ignore You
subtitle: Why Skills Trump Passion in the Quest for Work You Love
author: Cal Newport
description: Cal Newport debunks the passion hypothesis and argues that working right trumps finding the right work. Build rare and valuable skills instead of following your passion.

title: The Untethered Soul
subtitle: The Journey Beyond Yourself
author: Michael A. Singer
description: Michael Singer explores the question of who we are and takes us on a profound journey of self-discovery, helping us let go of limiting thoughts and emotions.

title: Mastery
author: Robert Greene
description: Robert Greene examines the lives of great masters — from Darwin to da Vinci — and distills the timeless strategies they used to achieve supreme levels of mastery in their fields.

title: Show Your Work!
subtitle: 10 Ways to Share Your Creativity and Get Discovered
author: Austin Kleon
description: Austin Kleon teaches you how to think about your work as a never-ending process, how to share your process in a way that attracts people, and how to deal with the ups and downs of putting yourself out there.

title: When Breath Becomes Air
author: Paul Kalanithi
description: A neurosurgeon faces terminal cancer and grapples with what makes life meaningful. His memoir is a profoundly moving, exquisitely observed reflection on the juncture of life and death.

title: The Power of Your Subconscious Mind
author: Joseph Murphy
description: Dr. Joseph Murphy reveals the enormous power of your subconscious mind and teaches practical techniques to harness it for success, health, and personal fulfillment.

title: Crushing It!
subtitle: How Great Entrepreneurs Build Their Business and Influence
author: Gary Vaynerchuk
description: Gary Vaynerchuk shows how entrepreneurs and influencers have built extraordinary personal brands and businesses using social media, and how you can do the same.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Between life and death there is a library. Nora Seed finds herself in the Midnight Library, where each book gives her the chance to live a different version of her life.

title: Principles
subtitle: Life and Work
author: Ray Dalio
description: Ray Dalio shares the unconventional principles he's developed over his career as the founder of Bridgewater Associates, one of the world's largest hedge funds.

title: Smarter Faster Better
subtitle: The Transformative Power of Real Productivity
author: Charles Duhigg
description: Charles Duhigg explores the eight key concepts behind true productivity — from motivation and goal setting to focus and decision making.

title: The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari
author: Robin Sharma
description: Robin Sharma tells the story of Julian Mantle, a lawyer forced to confront the spiritual crisis of his out-of-balance life and his subsequent odyssey to an ancient culture.

title: Tiny Habits
subtitle: The Small Changes That Change Everything
author: BJ Fogg
description: Stanford behavior scientist BJ Fogg reveals a breakthrough method for building habits that stick: start tiny, feel good, and grow from there.

title: The Art of Possibility
subtitle: Transforming Professional and Personal Life
author: Rosamund Stone Zander & Benjamin Zander
description: The Zanders combine Ben's experience as the conductor of the Boston Philharmonic with Rosamund's expertise as a psychotherapist to present practices for bringing about possibility in all human endeavors.
